entry.title: 🚀 Understanding Change Project Execution in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
🚀 Transporting Configuration Between Tenants in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2: A Change Project Guide

With SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 (V2), SAP continues its modernisation to reinvent the CRM experience—offering a cleaner, more modern interface and a scalable architecture designed for enterprise-level growth. As organizations adopt V2, one topic that frequently surfaces for implementation teams is how to reliably transport configuration changes between environments, particularly from Test (QA) to Production.

In this blog, we’ll walk through the essentials of executing a Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud V2, with a special focus on transporting configuration specifically for Case and Case Type services. You’ll learn how the process works, why it’s needed, and the best practices for ensuring a smooth transportation of changes.

🔍 What Is a Change Project in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2?

A Change Project is SAP’s structured way of capturing and transporting configuration changes across environments. It allows you to:

  • Record configuration updates in a designated source system (such as a staging or development tenant).
  • Transport these validated changes into target systems like Test or Production.
  • Use a multi-tier setup where one source tenant can push changes to multiple targets for the same customer ID.

In simple terms, think of a Change Project as a secure container holding all tested and approved configuration changes—ensuring that only validated configurations make their way to Production.

🔄 The Two-Step Transport Process

Transporting configuration in SAP Sales Cloud V2 happens in two key phases:

1️⃣ Initial Load

Before delta transports can be used, the system requires that all connected tenants — or any tenants that intend to participate in Change Project transports — are properly synchronised.

This is where the Initial Load comes in.

Here’s what happens during this phase (Source To Target):

  • The initial load ensures that configuration data for the chosen service (e.g., Case or Case Type) is identical across the source and target tenants.
  • It must be triggered by the customer because certain pre-checks or preparations may be required in the source environment.
  • By default, the initial load takes the current configuration from the Source and applies it to the Target tenant(s).
  • After completion, both tenants are fully aligned, with no extra or missing configuration.

Note: For most existing customers, the initial load is typically performed from the Production tenant to the Test tenant. This approach is considered safer because it does not pose any risk to the Production environment. Therefore, this direction of initial load—from PROD to TEST—will also be supported.

Once the Initial Load is done, you’re ready to move on to continuous change recording.

2️⃣ Delta Load

After synchronisation, any configuration changes you make in the source system are:

  • Automatically captured within the currently checked-out Change Project.
  • Stored as delta changes.
  • Transportable whenever the administrator decides to push them to the target environments.

This offers flexibility and control—only the changes you want, when you want them.

🔄  Understanding the Initial Load Process

When you trigger the Initial Load, the system collects all existing configuration data for the selected service—even if those configurations were created before the Change Project existed. Keep in mind that the Initial Load must be executed individually for each service.

In this blog, we’ll focus on Case and Case Type services.

🗂️  What Gets Transported?

Case: The following code list configurations are included when transporting Case data:

  1. Status Dictionary Entries
  2. Status Schemas
  3. Priorities
  4. Source Settings

Case Type: Transport inclusions for Case Type include:

  1. Case Type Code List
  2. Case Type Template Versions
  3. Action Catalog

These two services are registered separately so that dependent and independent configurations remain logically separated.

🔗 Case Type Service Dependencies

The Case Type service depends on several other services. Initial Load for Case Type can only proceed after the Initial Load of all dependent services is completed.

Dependent Services:

  • Approval
  • Autoflow
  • Mashup
  • Extensibility
  • Form Admin
  • Data Connector

This ensures that all necessary underlying data is already present in the target tenant before Case Type configurations are transported.

Before proceeding with Initial Load for Case or Case Type, make sure to go through the Important Considerations and Initial Load Behaviour sections.

Now, let’s walk through the Initial Load and Delta Load processes.


🚀 Executing the Initial Load & Delta Load

🔎 Step-by-Step: Initial Load for Case

Step 1: Start Initial Load (Case)

After configuring source and target tenants as mentioned in Change Project, initiate the Initial Load for the Case service by navigating to Settings → All Settings → Change Project → Administration → Registered Services → Case → Start Initial Load. Confirm the pop-up.

A Change Project titled “Initial Load for Case” is automatically created in the Source tenant.
This captures all configuration items related to Status Dictionary Entries, Status Schema, Origin (Source Settings) and Priority

The Change Project will appear with the status WORK IN PROGRESS, meaning it’s ready to be published.


Step 2: Publish the Change Project (Source Tenant)

In the Test (source) tenant, Login and navigate to:

Settings → All Settings → Change Project → Administration → Initial Load for Case → Publish.

Step 3: Transport the Change Project

Continue in the Test tenant:

Settings → All Settings → Change Project → Administration → Initial Load for Case → Transport.

You will be prompted to select the target tenants. Choose the appropriate ones and proceed. In my scenario, I had two target tenants.

After transporting, the change project status in the source system changes to Transported.

Step 4: Activate in Target Tenant

Log in to the Production (target) tenant:

Settings → All Settings → Change Project → Administration → All Change Projects

You will see the Initial Load for Case project with the status Imported.

Once you're satisfied with the incoming configurations, click Publish to activate the changes.

Step-5: 🔁 Performing Initial Load for Case Type

Since Case Type depends on other services, you must first complete Initial Loads for all dependent services.

The system enforces this and hides any services whose Initial Loads are already complete.

🧪 Step-by-Step Behaviour for Case Type

Let’s assume that initial load is done From Target to Source for Case Type.

Since the steps for performing the Initial Load for Case Type are nearly identical to those for Case, I won’t repeat them here. Instead, I’ll focus on explaining the Template Version concept in Case Type, which requires a more detailed walkthrough.

Step 1 — Before Initial Load

Case Type ZABC exists in both source (Quality) and target (Production), with versions as shown in the pre-load state.

Step 2 — After Initial Load (Prod → Source)

After performing the Initial Load from Production to Quality

Here CHT Means Change Transport.

  • The Source DRAFT version is removed, and the Target DRAFT version becomes the new DRAFT in the source tenant.
  • All existing template versions in the source are moved to INACTIVE status, and the prefix “CHT” is added to each version that is deactivated.
  • All incoming template versions from the target—whether ACTIVE, DRAFT, or INACTIVE—are created in the source exactly as they exist in the target.

This aligns both tenants to the same configuration baseline and visually user can co-relate the versions going forward.

🎉You’ve now completed the Case and Case Type Initial Load and successfully transported configurations between the tenants in SAP Sales Cloud V2!


🚀 Delta Load

When Delta Load is enabled:

  • Any configuration changes made to Case or Case Type in the source tenant are automatically captured in the checked out Change Project—this happens when the project is checked out and the changes are performed. If a Change Project is not checked out, you will be getting an error as below.

     


    • Solution: Go to the Change Project list and either create a new Delta-type change project (which will automatically check it out for your logged-in user) or open an existing open change project and check it out. This step ensures the system can track and store your delta changes correctly.

  • When the administrator decides to transport these updates or the delta change projects  to the target tenant, the captured changes are transported and published based on the admin’s choice.
  • Since Case configuration handling is straightforward, I won’t go through those steps here.
  • Case Type however, behaves differently. After the delta Change Project is transported and published in the Production (target) tenant, the template version statuses are updated as follows with continuing above example. 
    • Version 4 transitions from DRAFT → ACTIVE
    • Version 3 transitions from ACTIVE → INACTIVE

⚠️ Important Considerations

When you perform the Initial Load, the system imports all existing configuration for the selected service—including configurations created before the Change Project existed.

As a result:

  • The Initial Load must be executed separately for each service.
  • Case and Case Type each require their own independent Initial Load.
  • The order is important:
    Run Initial Load for Case Service first, and then run Initial Load for Case Type. 
  • Since Catalog is a master data entity and is referenced within Case Type configurations, it’s important that customers maintain the same Catalog Display ID across all tenants before implementing Change Projects for Case Type. The system relies on matching Catalog Display IDs between the source and target tenants when validating and transporting data, and this alignment is required for both the Initial Load and Delta Load processes for Case Type.

  • Additional points:

    • Service Levels are not included in the Change Project scope.
    • Decision Table Configurations—Configurations such as Service Level Determination, Case Routing to Employee, and Case Routing to Team are not transported through Change Projects as they may contain tenant specific data. Also they support import/export using excel.
    • However, the decision table ID maintained against Case Routing to Team and Case Routing to Employee within the Case Template version is transported. You only need to recreate the corresponding decision table in the target tenant using the same DisplayId.
    • The Number Range name is included in the Change Project for Case Types; however, the actual number range values may differ between source and target tenants. 
      • Hence, In the target tenant - the number range remains editable under:
        Settings → Case Management → Number Range.

In this blog series, we’ll walk through how to execute these steps specifically for Case and Case Type configurations in SAP Sales Cloud V2.

🔄 Initial Load Behaviour

Before initiating the Initial Load, it’s important to understand how the target tenant will react to incoming configuration if the initial load is done in Source to Target Direction:

  • New configuration in the target → will be added
  • Existing configuration (common) → will be updated
  • Configuration missing in the source but present in the target → will be deleted

Before any deletion occurs, the system performs a usage check.

If a configuration item is actively used in Case transactions or referenced by other configurations, the system will fail the Initial Load, indicating that the item cannot be removed.

✅ Best Practices 

  • Transporting Deletions:  When transporting deleted configurations between tenants—typically during a delta transport—make sure to first verify in the target tenant whether the configuration you plan to delete is actively used. You can quickly check this by using Case OWL Advanced Filters, which provide visibility into all configuration fields related to both Case and Case Type.

🛠️ Admin Options When Conflicts Occur

If an Initial Load fails because a configuration is still in use, administrators generally have two options to resolve the issue. Below, we’ll walk through these options using an example where the Initial Load direction is Source → Target.
  1. Recreate the configuration in the source tenant
    Usually, delete conflict in target occurs If a configuration cannot be deleted due to transactional dependencies. If that's the case recreate the same configuration in the source tenant and then retry the Initial Load.

  2. Request a fresh Target Tenant copy as Source. 
    You can request a clean tenant copy for the target environment (typically a fresh copy of Production) and perform the Initial Load on this new instance—this is especially useful for customers who are already live in Production.

This avoids configuration conflicts and ensures the Initial Load executes smoothly.

🟦 Conclusion

Transporting configurational data in SAP Sales Cloud V2 is no longer a complex or risky activity when approached through a well‑structured Change Project process. By understanding how Initial Load, Delta Load, and service dependencies work—especially for Case and Case Type—you gain full control over how configuration moves across tenants.

With Change Projects acting as controlled containers, administrators decide what gets packaged, when it gets transported, and how it is activated in the target environment. This ensures that Production receives only the changes that have been reviewed, tested, and intentionally transported.

As organizations continue adopting SAP Sales Cloud V2, mastering this transport mechanism becomes essential for maintaining stability, consistency, and governance across environments. By following the steps and considerations outlined in this guide, you can confidently execute configuration transports with precision and predictability.

If you're looking to explore more advanced scenarios, best practices with Change Projects in V2, stay tuned for upcoming posts!

P.S : If you run into any issues during the process or need assistance with implementation, please raise an incident with SAP.

entry.title: Supplier reconciliation line is split by profit center and tax characteristics in supplier invoice
entry.content:
During supplier invoice posting, the supplier reconciliation (vendor) line item is split into multiple lines instead of remaining as a single consolidated open item. The splitting occurs based on document splitting characteristics such as profit center and tax-related account assignments.

This can lead to unexpected document structures during Accounts Payable review and reporting.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Supplier reconciliation line is split by profit cente...
entry.content:
This behavior is standard and working as designed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Document splitting logic is applied during supplier invoice posting to ensure balanced postings and consistent account assignment reporting in the General Ledger. Supplier line items are split based on the related expense and tax items.

The system internally considers account assignment characteristics such as profit center, segment, and tax-related information when determining the supplier line representation in the ledger view. Therefore, invoices containing multiple account assignment combinations may result in multiple supplier reconciliation line items.

SAP KBA 3035677 explains the document splitting logic and notes that:

supplier items are split based on expense and tax items,
tax items are processed before supplier items,
tax code information is considered internally for follow-up accounting processes.

Currently, there is no standard configuration available to universally enforce a single supplier reconciliation line item per invoice when multiple document splitting characteristics exist.

entry.title: Standard Hierarchy for Cost Center is not available in Post General Journal Entries app
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Cost Center value help behaves differently across applications.

Some apps, such as Cash Journal, allow users to navigate and select Cost Centers using the Standard Hierarchy view. However, in the Post General Journal Entries app, the Cost Center value help only provides a flat search-based list without hierarchy navigation.

Users may expect consistent hierarchy-based selection behavior across apps and want to know whether this functionality can be enabled.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Standard Hierarchy for Cost Center is not available i...
entry.content:
This behavior is standard and depends on the application design and underlying value help implementation of each app.

The Cash Journal app supports hierarchy-based Cost Center navigation, while the Post General Journal Entries app uses a flat search help and does not currently support Standard Hierarchy navigation for Cost Center selection.

At present, there is no configuration available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to enable Standard Hierarchy selection in the Post General Journal Entries app.

As an alternative, users can search by Cost Center ID or description, or use apps that support hierarchy navigation when identifying the required Cost Center values.

If hierarchy-based selection is required in additional posting apps, a feature request should be submitted for evaluation.

entry.title: Manage Quality Certificate and SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hello SAP community! Today I would like to share a new feature to address a big common pain for a lot of customers related to managing quality certificates.

Handling incoming supplier certificates is often a manual task. These documents typically arrive separately via email and are stored as attachments on your desktop, sometimes in different formats. If necessary, they may be filed manually, requiring additional time to compare results and align data with quality inspections. This process is time-consuming and makes tracking difficult.

To address these challenges, the development team aims to minimize delays and reduce oversight of documents. This will allow us to focus on more important activities. With the release of 2025 version, a new Fiori application for managing incoming quality certificates is now available!

The new app is named Manage Certificate Receipts, which allows you to centralize this process, providing users a chance to record certificates, track their status, and view details all in one single place. The app is directly linked to goods receipt, allowing us to cleanly assign certificates to purchase orders. The main objective is to serve a management tool to monitor the receipt of quality certificates from suppliers, ensuring that each required certificate is received for the respective materials and stored in DMS. You can define the necessity of these certificates in the material master and track their receipt independently of the goods receipt through various methods, including manual recording and automatic system updates.

As you can see in the figure, the app displays the list of Certificates based of filter criteria such as certificate type, status, material, purchasing document, etc...  From the list, you can deep dive into the object page for each one to view the certificate receipts details

From the worklist you can also create a manual certificate receipts, which is a feature to enable the creation via API (Quality Certificate Receipt - Create, Update (A2X)), by the system or just manually for example for those cases when you are receiving the certificate even before the GR is posted. You can find more information here: Creating Certificate Receipts

The effect is greater transparency and less manual work.!!

But the magic really happens when If SAP Document AI is integrated in S/4HANA Public Cloud that will enable a new option of 'start certificate upload' to directly navigate to the Upload Quality Certificate Documents app, to upload certificates and see the full list of all the uploaded documents from there... With this functionality you will use the AI-assisted processing of incoming quality certificates and accelerate the confirmation of certificates and speeding up the entire process of inspection and stock release. All you have to do is to upload the document from your desk, and AI will take care of matching the information; the system support several formats such as PDF  and JPEG!!

As you can see in the figure above, the document has different statuses, after uploading, first you will note the In progress status, meaning that the system is processing and reading the documentation and extract all the data in the background, once ready it will change to Ready for Posting and immediately after to confirmed, meaning that at this point of time, you can see the certificate link generated to display the object page and to review the information uploaded and administrative data relevant. Besides and most importantly, the system creates a certificate matched where you will be able to review the collected data on the background,  validate the details of log messages and make adjustments or solve inconsistencies in case needed. On the figure below you see how is possible to compare the information from the document uploaded vs the data collected after matching the information to approve, edit or complete the match:

If you are interest of knowing more about this app and the new AI features, please check the following links:

Manage Certificate Receipts - SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

Quality Certificates for Goods Receipt | SAP Help Portal

Manage Certificate Receipts | SAP Help Portal

Integration of SAP Document AI | SAP Help Portal

SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, processing of incoming quality certificates with SAP Document AI

Note: The integration of uploading a quality certificate document using SAP Document AI, is currently planned in the QM roadmap to be released by Q3/Q4 of 2026 for Private cloud edition as well, meantime remains available only for public cloud...

Hope you find the information relevant for your business need!

Enjoy!

entry.title: Deferred Revenue in SAP Convergent Invoicing: The Overlooked Alternative to RAR
entry.content:
1. Introduction

In SAP BRIM projects, revenue recognition requirements often lead toward SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR), and in many cases for a good reason. RAR is designed to meet accounting standards such as IFRS 15 and US GAAP ASC 606, where recognition is driven by performance obligations, contract-based accounting, and transaction price allocation, rather than just invoicing.

But there is another standard capability within Convergent Invoicing (CI) and FI-CA that is often overlooked during solution design, called time-based deferred revenue processing. In the right scenarios, it provides a simple way to handle unearned revenue over time, without introducing an additional revenue accounting subledger that comes with RAR.

To be clear upfront, CI Deferred Revenue is not a replacement for RAR. It does not cover IFRS 15 / US GAAP ASC 606 compliance, and it is not designed for performance obligation based recognition.

Instead, it is a CI/FI-CA capability for time-based revenue deferral, where revenue is recognised at invoicing, then reclassified and released to the P&L over time based on a predefined schedule.

This works well with high-volume subscription billing scenarios where the revenue patterns are known upfront and there is no need for complex allocation logic.

 

2. Technical implementation

This article focuses on time-based deferral used in subscription-based BRIM scenarios where revenue is recognised over predefined schedule defined at invoicing.
For completeness, CI also supports event-based deferral, which is used in consumption-based billing models where revenue recognition is triggered by business events rather than a fixed schedule.

 

2.1. Scenario

A customer buys a 12 month subscription for USD 1,200, billed upfront.

  • Billing period: 15 Apr 2026 – 14 Apr 2027
  • Posting date: 15 Apr 2026
  • Total invoice: USD 1,200
  • Monthly revenue: USD 100

What happens from an accounting perspective?

At invoice posting, the full amount is billed and recognised in revenue. The unearned portion is then reclassified to deferred revenue and released monthly to the P&L over the duration of the subscription.

 

Line

Apr 26

May 26

Jun 26

Jul 26

Aug 26

Sep 26

Oct 26

Nov 26

Dec 26

Jan 27

Feb 27

Mar 27

Receivable (Dr)

1,200

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Unearned (Cr)

-1,100

-1000

-900

-800

-700

-600

-500

-400

-300

-200

-100

0

Revenue (Cr)

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

-100

Figure 1 Revenue Schedule 

 

2.2 Configuration

The configuration activities are straightforward, coming down to three steps.

 Activate deferred revenue interface in BIT

This enables the transfer of deferred revenue related data into BIT and activates CI deferred revenue processing.

Figure 2 BIT Interface component

 

Enable deferred revenue in the invoicing process

Assign the invoicing function DEF_REVENUES to the relevant invoicing process.

SPRO-> Financial Accounting -> Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable -> Business Transactions -> Convergent Invoicing   -> Invoicing  -> Invoicing Processes  -> Define Invoicing Processes

Figure 3 SPRO: Maintain invoicing function

 

Configure deferred revenue postings

Define account determination (mapping deferred revenue to revenue GL accounts) and FI-CA document type used for the transfer posting.

SPRO-> Financial Accounting -> Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable -> Business Transactions -> Deferred Revenue Postings

Figure 4 SPRO: Deferred Revenue Configuration  

2.3 FI-CA Event 2615

This is the most important part. Event 2615 is where all the revenue recognition logic is defined. At runtime, the event receives billing, invoicing, and FI-CA document data as input and returns a revenue recognition schedule with dates and amounts. The final schedule is stored in the table FKKDEFREV and processed via mass activity T-code FPDR.

Figure 5 Sample FM in event 2615    

 

In simple terms, Event 2615 is where you translate business rules into a fixed schedule for revenue recognition.

Functional design decisions:  

When designing the logic, the following areas must be defined:  

  • Item filtering logic: define which billing types and products are in scope. Typically, one-off charges or non-subscription items are excluded.
  • Period determination:  define how contract periods are derived from billing dates and how partial months are handled at the start and end of the contract.
  • Rounding logic:  define how rounding differences are handled. Usually differences are assigned to the last period.
  • Error handling and fallback behaviour: define system behaviour when schedule cannot be created.

Real world complexity

Our example is quite simple and straightforward to implement. But real implementation quickly introduces additional scenarios, including:

  • Mid-contract changes like additions of services, price adjustments, or credit memos
  • Pre-billing scenarios where invoicing occurs before the billing period starts
  • Early termination where contracts end earlier then planned

These scenarios directly impact the revenue recognition schedule and must be properly handled in Event 2615. In practice, this is where most implementation effort sits. It is far better to capture these during design than to discover them during build or testing.

 

2. 4 Processing (FPDR)

When the schedule is created, the revenue transfer is executed by transaction FPDR. This is typically scheduled as a batch job. The program reads entries from FKKDEFREV, selects based on date and posts accounting entries (FI-CA Documents).

  Figure 6 Mass Activity – Deferred Revenue Processing    

 FPDR does not recalculate anything, it simply reads and executes the schedule generated in event 2615.

 

2.5 Accounting View

For the 12-month subscription example, the postings look like this:

 1. Invoice Posting

Dr Receivables – 1,200 / Cr Revenue 1,200

Full amount billed and recognised at invoice posting.

2. Deferral Revenue Posting

Dr Revenue 1,100 / Cr Deferred Revenue 1,100

Unearned portion reclassified to the balance sheet. USD 100 is retained as April's earned revenue.

3. Monthly Recognition (recurring)

Dr Deferred Revenue 100 / Cr Revenue 100

USD 100 released to P&L each month for the remaining 11 periods.

 

  Figure 7 Deferred Revenue Processing   - Accounting View

 

3. Conclusion

Convergent Invoicing time-based deferred revenue is an efficient capability for handling revenue recognition in subscription-driven, high-volume environments where recognition schedules are predictable.

It provides separation of earned and unearned revenue, and automates recognition over time without the complexity of a full SAP RAR implementation.

Final takeaway

CI deferred revenue does not calculate revenue recognition. Rather it executes a predefined schedule created during invoicing.

It should not be positioned as a simplified alternative to SAP RAR, but as a separate FI-CA accounting capability for a time-based revenue deferral. It is simply a different accounting mechanism.

Where revenue is strictly time-based and there is no need for performance obligation-based allocation, FI-CA deferred revenue is often an appropriate design choice in SAP BRIM.

 

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not represent official SAP guidance. The examples and approaches are simplified to illustrate possible technical implementation. This is not a complete solution for production use. Do not apply any solution in a production system without fully testing and confirming it in a non-production environment.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports PDF bookmark displays incorrect Japan...
entry.content:
Thank you for the comment.

To clarify our situation further, the bookmarks are not defined in the rpt file itself.
In our implementation, the rpt does not contain any bookmark settings.

Bookmarks are generated dynamically at runtime by the application using
ReportDataSet / GroupSubReport (AddGroupSubReportRow),
and the bookmark text is provided programmatically.

Because of this approach, sharing an rpt file alone (even with saved data)
would not reproduce the issue, as the bookmark values are injected
by the application logic during report execution.

Due to security policies, we cannot share application source code,
but we can describe the behavior and structure in more detail
or help verify whether this usage pattern has known limitations.

Please let us know how you would like us to proceed.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports PDF bookmark displays incorrect Japanese character (名 → 吊)
entry.content:
Thanks for the clarification.

For additional context, we are currently using
SAP Crystal Reports 2020 (SP37, 64-bit) for both development and runtime.

From a project constraint perspective, upgrading to a newer major version
(e.g. CR 2025) is not an option as a direct resolution,
even if the issue does not reproduce there.
Major version upgrades have compatibility and operational implications,
so the current version must remain unchanged.

That said, we understand that validating the behavior on a newer version
can still be useful for classification purposes
(e.g. known issue vs. limitation vs. fixed behavior),
and potentially for determining whether the issue could be reviewed by R&D.

Our primary goal is to clarify whether this behavior:
- is a known limitation or defect in Crystal Reports 2020, and
- has any recommended workaround or configuration within the same version.

We will consider whether a trial-based verification on a newer version
is feasible purely for comparison, and will share the outcome if available.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: Dealing with multiple currencies in one business process
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a case where a Vietnamese company buys raw material from a Japanese supplier in JPY. Meaning the purchase order and invoice is in JPY. They are using USD bank account to negotiate with the house bank to buy JPY. They will purchase a forward foreign exchange hedging option for USD/JPY.

December 1, 2025: Purchase order for JPY 32,600,000, negotiated with the house bank to purchase forward foreign exchange at an exchange rate of 153.53. USD 212,336.35 * 153.53 = JPY 32,600,000

March 1, 2026: The total PO to be paid to Japanese supplier is JPY 36,374,845, of which JPY 32,600,000 was already purchased with house bank on December 1, 2025 as forward foreign exchange in JPY. Additionally, JPY 3,774,845 was not hedged with a forward foreign exchange contract. Therefore, on March 1, 2026, they negotiate the exchange rate with house bank for this purchase order JPY 3,774,845 without forward foreign exchange hedging. USD 24080.41 * 156.76 = JPY 3,774,845.

Finally, on March 1, 2026, a total of USD 236,416.76 will be deducted and converted into JPY 36,374,845, which will be remitted to Japanese supplier.

In this case, as Vietnamese company code currency is VND, while transaction currency code is JPY. What is the best practice to handle the payment in this case, given our SAP system doesn't have TRM scope item, only have FI scope item. If I use Manage Automatic Payment app, SAP is going to only consider the the JPY/VND exchange rate and pay the invoice in JPY as transaction currency. There's no way to control USD/JPY in SAP, as my current understanding.

Please help guide me on this matter. Thank you for your reading time.

entry.title: Re: SES rejected in Ariba -is the supplier notified?
entry.content:
I would like to ask a follow-up question related to ERS for service procurement, to confirm my understanding and learn from others’ experience.

For suppliers that are enabled for ERS:

2) Once the invoice is created, does the buyer need to take any further action, or is the process completely automatic?

My current understanding (based on SAP documentation such as SINV-6042) is that ERS can be used for service procurement in the same way as for materials, and no additional action is required from the buyer side after final SES approval. However, I have not implemented this in a real project yet, so I would really appreciate confirmation or insights from those with hands-on experience.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Your Feedback Matters - Multi-language captions for SAP Learning
entry.content:
We have exciting news for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers - and we need your input to make it even better!

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team are thrilled to announce that we now have the capability to provide multi-language captions for our SAP Learning videos. This is a big step forward in making our many how-to videos on SAP Learning more accessible, inclusive, and impactful for learners around the globe.

These how-to videos are provided supplementary to SAP Learning Journeys and SAP Learning Courses. They bring you deeper how-to expertise with demonstrations on targeted topics.  The aim is to speed up your innovation adoption.

You can see an example of this on Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – which is now available with captions in English, German, Spanish, and Portuguese (Brazil).

You choose your caption language Captions in the Settings of SAP Learning video player as shown on the video Getting Started with Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - German language is selected and German captions can be seen overlaying the video

But here's the thing — we want YOU to help us prioritize.

Why Multi-Language Captions Matter

Learning complex enterprise software like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is challenging enough without the added barrier of a language gap. Whether you're an end user, a consultant, or an IT professional, being able to follow along in your preferred language can make all the difference between confusion and confidence.

Multi-language captions mean:

  • Greater accessibility for non-native English speakers
  • Better knowledge retention when learning in your native language
  • Wider reach and easier reuse for SAP Learning content
  • A more inclusive learning experience for everyone

Which languages would you like to see?

This is just as important as the content itself! Whether it's German, French, Spanish, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Portuguese, or another language entirely — your feedback will help us shape our roadmap.

Where Do You Want Us to Start?

With dozens of SAP Learning videos already created for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we want to focus first on what matters most to you. Here are some areas we're considering — but we'd love your thoughts!

Joule Videos

Are the videos covering Joule, SAP's AI copilot, at the top of your list? As AI-driven features become increasingly central to the SAP experience, understanding Joule in your own language could significantly enhance adoption and productivity across your organization.

For example:

  • Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Resolving Missing Business Roles for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

These supplement Joule courses such as New Course: Getting Started with Joule for Business Users and Course: Introducing Joule 

More videos coming soon!

Fiori Videos

Do Fiori videos take priority for you? With SAP Fiori being the primary user interface for SAP S/4HANA, having captions in multiple languages could help countless users navigate/configure/activate/extend/support and make the most of SAP Fiori launchpad, apps, and classic UIs. For example:

Introductions for Business Users, Key Users, and Consultants:

  • Introducing SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Creating SAP Fiori Launchpad Layouts for Custom Business Roles
  • Extending SAP Fiori Object Pages with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori List Report Apps with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori Overview Pages with Adapt UI

How-to guidance for Administrators, Architects, and Support teams:

  • Patching SAP Fiori (SAPUI5)
  • Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List
  • Configuring the SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA
  • Bringing Apps Onto SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • Activating SAP Fiori Content in Custom Business Roles
  • Enabling the 'Share: Microsoft Teams' Functionality for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA
  • Integrating Contact and Presence Info from Microsoft for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Learning Journey: SAP Fiori - System Administration and Learning courses such as Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori and Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics

Other Videos

Is there another category of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition videos that you feel is more urgently needed? Is there a video you need that you would like to see created? For example, topics in:

  • Finance and Controlling
  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Enterprise Asset Management
  • System Administration
  • Configuring Integration with other Cloud solutions
  • Using SAP Activate for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Projects

Some examples:

  • Configuring Situation Handling for Maintenance Task Lists in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Implementing Cross-App Navigation Control for Fiori Applications (“Open In” Capability) in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Enabling Advanced Shipping & Receiving Capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS0
  • Setting up the Data Replication Framework for Product Replication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Sales/Service Cloud Version 2
  • Mastering the Planning and Execution of a Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (exclusive content)

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement, Implementing Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and many more.

What's most valuable to your role and your organization?

This is your opportunity to directly influence what we bring as SAP Learning how-to videos to you and your teams.

Share your Feedback

Please take a moment to share your thoughts by answering these quick questions in the comments below:

  1. Which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition video topics are your top priority for multi-language captions?
  2. Which specific languages are most important to you or your organization?
  3. Is there anything else you'd like us to consider as we start to add captions.
  4. Do you have proposals for new videos we can consider for our roadmap ?

Every comment counts. Whether you're a customer, an SAP partner, an independent learner or an employee — your voice helps us build a better learning experience for everyone.

Drop your thoughts in the comments below — we can't wait to hear from you!

Becoming a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Guru

Follow our tag S4HANA RIG - SAP Community for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

Find more of our blogs, videos, whitepapers and other accelerators in our SAP Community topic pages:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA inconsistencies
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 

For intermittent issue with incremental load, usually caused by something in the server.  Either network down or maintenance or something else.  I don't know what is happening on that specific day and time.  You will need to create support ticket if you want to dig deeper.

Same thing with "IsConsumerInterrupted" message, there could be something happening in the server and any process need to be terminated.  As for the "IsCancelled" it seems you trigger the initial load multiple times consecutively based on the time in the log and only one initial load can run at a time.

Thank you.

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - Just Ask
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能をつかってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、「データを確認したいが、ダッシュボードを開いて目的のチャートを探すのが手間」「欲しいグラフを作るためにストーリー編集の知識が必要で自分では対応できない」という場合に自然言語でデータに関して質問するだけで、即座にシンプルなチャートおよびテーブルとして回答を得ることができる機能、Just Askについてご紹介いたします。

目次：

  • Just Askの概要
  • Just Askの利用方法[ハンズオン]

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画⾯キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年5⽉に作成しています。）

Just Askの概要

SAP Analytics CloudのJust Ask機能は、自然言語で質問するだけでデータを可視化・検索することができる自然言語クエリ機能です。欲しいデータにすぐにアクセスすることや、SAP Analytics Cloudの操作やストーリーの作成知識がなくてもデータへのアクセスを行うことに役立ちます。

※2026年4月現在、英語、スペイン語、イタリア語、およびドイツ語でのみサポートされています。
※日本語は正式にサポートされていませんが動作する事もあります。是非お試しください！

詳しくはHelp Portalもご参照ください：Just Ask を使用したデータの探索

 

Just Askの利用方法

1. Just Askを起動しよう！

1.1. 画面右上のメニューバーの「電球」ボタンをクリックして起動、またはホーム画面のタイルからJust Ask機能にアクセスします。
※Just Ask機能がテナントで有効化されていない場合などにはメニューが表示されない可能性があります。Just Ask機能が利用できない場合にはSAP Analytics Cloudの管理者にご確認ください。

 

1.2 画面上部のメニューバーからアクセスした場合には、Just Askのスタート画面が表示されます。

 

2. Just Askを使って分析しよう！

1.1. 質問をしたいデータモデルを選択します。

必要なデータモデルが見つからない場合には、「検索に別のモデルを追加」からSAP Analytics Cloud のファイルリポジトリまたはSAP Datasphere のモデルを追加し、選択することができます。

 

1.2 データに関する質問を自然言語で入力します。あらかじめ管理者が設定したサンプル質問を利用することや入力補助機能を使った入力も可能です。

 

1.3 入力した質問に基づき、Just Askが自動的にデータを解析してチャートや集計値として結果を表示します。必要に応じて再度質問をしたり、質問を追加します。

 

1.4. チャートで表示されたデータをテーブル形式に表示を切り替えたり、フィルタやソート、ドリルダウン、データのエクスポート、データアナライザ（アドホックな分析機能）の起動などの操作を行うことも可能です。

 

1.5. チャートの編集をクリックし、チャートの種類やメジャー、ディメンション等を変更し、分析を深める事も可能です。

 

今回はJust Ask 機能のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

<timeStatistics/>
</component>
<component dplorder="-1" size="0" vendor="sap.com" name="tc/odata4j/primary" description="The item is skipped as there are items which depend on it. The items which depend on are: (name: 'tc~idm~rest~ear', vendor: 'sap.com' ; name: 'tc~idm~ui5~ear', vendor: 'sap.com')" location="SAP AG" type="DC" version="na" status="SKIPPED" csn="na">
<timeStatistics/>
</component>
</DeploymentResult>

I already tried regenerating a new stack but the error is still the same.

Any idea how to solve it?

Thank you.

#Javaupgrade

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Subject: Challenge Completed – AI Developer Challenge April 2026: Build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub

Hi Nora and Kevin,

Thank you for organizing this excellent 4-week AI Developer Challenge! It was a very engaging and hands-on learning journey into Agentic AI, RAG architectures, orchestration workflows, and enterprise AI development within the SAP BTP ecosystem.

I’m happy to share that I successfully completed all four weeks of the challenge. Some of my key learnings and highlights include:

✅ Week 1 – Implemented Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using SAP Generative AI Hub orchestration workflows and document grounding capabilities.

✅ Week 2 – Built my first AI agent using CrewAI integrated with SAP Generative AI Hub foundation models to process and analyze social welfare inquiries.

✅ Week 3 – Enhanced the agent with a custom grounding tool connected to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, enabling autonomous retrieval of context-aware and grounded information.

✅ Week 4 – Explored the SAP-RPT-1 foundation model for structured/tabular data predictions and gained insights into transformer-based enterprise AI for classification and regression scenarios.

This challenge provided valuable practical experience across SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, CrewAI, grounding services, vector databases, orchestration workflows, and predictive AI models.

Really appreciate the effort put into designing such a well-structured and beginner-friendly challenge. Looking forward to more SAP Community AI challenges and learning opportunities in the future!

Best regards,
Kenzi Sibuma

entry.title: Moving Legacy On-Premise Console Apps Scheduling & Monitoring to SAP CPI ?
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently evaluating the best architecture to integrate and monitor our legacy on-premise console applications (e.g., C# / Python scripts doing data processing and DB syncs).

 

Our Main Goal: We want proactive monitoring. If a job fails or a DB connection drops, IT should get an alert immediately, rather than waiting for business users to complain.

 

Currently, they are scheduled via Windows Task Scheduler. We are debating between two architectural approaches and would love your expert opinions:

 

Approach A (Initial Thought): Move Scheduling to SAP CPI

 

  • Wrap the legacy console apps into Web APIs.

  • Use SAP Cloud Connector to expose them.

  • Use SAP CPI (Cloud Integration) Timer to trigger these APIs every 10 minutes.

  • Reasoning: If the API fails, CPI will log an error, and we can monitor the iFlows.

 

Approach B (Proposed Alternative): Keep Local Scheduling & Push to SAP Cloud ALM

 

  • Keep using Windows Task Scheduler (No Cloud Connector needed).

  • Add a few lines of code (or a wrapper script) to the console apps to push a "Heartbeat" (Status: OK / ERROR) to SAP Cloud ALM (Integration & Exception Monitoring / Custom Metric) via REST API after execution.

  • Set a "Missing Event" rule in Cloud ALM. If the app doesn't send an 'OK' signal within 15 mins (e.g., server crash), Cloud ALM sends an alert.

 

My Questions for the Community:

 

  1. Is Approach A considered an anti-pattern? Will polling an on-premise system every 10 mins generate too many unnecessary Message Processing Logs (MPL) and increase costs/overhead in CPI?

  2. Is Approach B the recommended best practice for pure monitoring purposes?

  3. Has anyone implemented Approach B, and are there any blind spots I should be aware of?

Thank you !

entry.title: QM qualitative MIC inspection result to batch characteristc value. (valuation prefix)
entry.content:
Hi All,

Scenario:

Qualitative inspection result (Grade B) is recorded and valuated.

Code = B and code text = "Grade B " (Catalog 1 is used)

Following the UD, when the result is transferred to the batch characteristic, it is saved as “A Grade B” instead of just “Grade B”

A = Accepted (valuation prefix)
Grade B = Selected qualitative value

Is this standard SAP behavior when transferring qualitative QM results to batch characteristics?

(Observed Standard logic = Valuation prefix + Code group text)

 

I'm looking for a way to record the batch characteristics without the valuation prefix.

Appreciate your input on this, thanks.
